what_it_does: Exports a React component that renders three icon buttons for switching the application's view mode between 'both', 'circle', and 'square'.
observations: Defines a local MODES array pairing each ViewMode with a label and an inline SVG glyph.
Uses a stamp() helper to attach ui://controls/view and ui://controls/view/{id} addresses to the container and each button.
Buttons are controlled by the view prop and call setView when clicked.
SVG glyphs are aria-hidden; each button has aria-label and title set to the mode label.
A top-of-file comment explains the circle-in-square metaphor for the three modes.
standouts: The inline SVG glyphs visually encode the conceptual model described in the comment (circle in square, circle only, square only).
Every interactive element is stamped with a stable ui://controls/view/{id} address.
The component is purely presentational and stateless; selection is driven by props.
conventions: React functional component with explicit props type.
Maps a configuration array to rendered buttons using key={m.id}.
Uses className modifier (viewtoggle-btn--on) for active state.
Provides accessibility labels on icon-only buttons.
concerns: The comment date '2026-06-14' is in the future relative to current knowledge, which may be a typo or placeholder.
stamp() spreads unknown props onto DOM elements; its behavior and safety are not visible in this file.
No keyboard navigation grouping (e.g., role='group' or roving tabindex) is provided for the three related toggle buttons.
notes: A small, symbolic view-mode switcher that ties its iconography to a meaning-vs-structure metaphor and stamps each control with a stable UI address.
questions: What does stamp() actually attach to the DOM, and are the generated attributes safe for HTML?
What are the exact values of the ViewMode type imported from '../App'?
How is the visual styling for .viewtoggle and .viewtoggle-btn supplied?
What is meant by 'the seed's one space' in the comment?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it lets the user toggle among three view modes using labeled icon buttons.
intention_signals: To give users a compact, self-describing control for choosing whether to see meaning and structure together or in isolation, with glyphs that make the relationship visually obvious.
novelty: The glyphs are designed as a tiny diagram of the underlying concept (circle inscribed in square) rather than generic icons, and each button is tagged with a stable ui:// address.
contribution: A reusable, accessible view-mode toggle component that couples visual metaphor with stable UI addressing, making the control both self-documenting and referenceable.
summary_for_embedding: A React toggle component that renders three inline-SVG icon buttons for switching between 'both', 'circle', and 'square' view modes, using stamped UI addresses and accessibility labels.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an accessible, visually self-describing control for switching between combined and separated meaning/structure views.

symbols.does: Renders a three-state view-mode toggle as accessible icon buttons.
symbols.description: ViewToggle is a controlled React component that receives the current ViewMode and a setter. It maps a local MODES configuration to buttons, applies an active-state class, and uses inline SVG glyphs plus stamped ui://controls/view/{id} addresses to let the user choose among 'both', 'circle', and 'square' views.
